questions.q: On what date was Bhagat Singh executed?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was hanged on 23 March 1931 in Lahore.

questions.q: In which city was Bhagat Singh executed?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was hanged in Lahore Central Jail in 1931.

questions.q: By what honorific title is Bhagat Singh widely remembered?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh is honoured as Shaheed-e-Azam, meaning the Great Martyr.

questions.q: Bhagat Singh was a member of which revolutionary organization?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was a leading member of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA).

questions.q: In which 1929 act did Bhagat Singh throw a bomb 'to make the deaf hear'?
options: Bombing of the Central Legislative Assembly
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh threw bombs in the Central Legislative Assembly in April 1929 to register protest.

questions.q: Which two comrades were executed along with Bhagat Singh on 23 March 1931?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was hanged alongside Rajguru and Sukhdev on 23 March 1931.

questions.q: Which slogan is most closely associated with Bhagat Singh?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh popularized the slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad', meaning 'Long Live the Revolution'.

questions.q: For the killing of which British police officer was Bhagat Singh primarily tried and sentenced?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was tried for the 1928 killing of Assistant Superintendent John Saunders.

questions.q: Approximately how old was Bhagat Singh when he was executed?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was about 23 years old when he was hanged in 1931.

questions.q: In which present-day country's region was Bhagat Singh born?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was born in 1907 in the village of Banga in the Punjab district of Lyallpur, now in Pakistan.

questions.q: Which comrade threw bombs in the Central Legislative Assembly alongside Bhagat Singh in 1929?
questions.explanation: Batukeshwar Dutt joined Bhagat Singh in the Assembly bombing and was arrested with him.

questions.q: The Saunders killing in 1928 was carried out to avenge the death of which leader?
questions.explanation: The killing of Saunders avenged Lala Lajpat Rai, who died after a lathi charge during a Simon Commission protest.

questions.q: Which youth organization did Bhagat Singh help found in 1926?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh co-founded the Naujawan Bharat Sabha in 1926 to spread revolutionary ideas among youth.

questions.q: Which essay written by Bhagat Singh in jail explained his rejection of religious belief?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh wrote the essay 'Why I Am an Atheist' while imprisoned in 1930.

questions.q: In which conspiracy case was Bhagat Singh tried and sentenced to death?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was sentenced in the Lahore Conspiracy Case relating to the Saunders killing.

questions.q: While in jail, Bhagat Singh and fellow prisoners undertook what form of protest demanding better treatment?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh led a long hunger strike in jail demanding that political prisoners be treated with dignity.

questions.q: Which fellow prisoner died during the 1929 jail hunger strike that Bhagat Singh supported?
questions.explanation: Jatindra Nath Das died after a 63-day hunger strike in 1929, a protest joined by Bhagat Singh.

questions.q: After the Assembly bombing, what did Bhagat Singh and his comrade deliberately do?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh and Dutt threw bombs that injured no one and then allowed themselves to be arrested to use the trial as a platform.

questions.q: What was the year of Bhagat Singh's birth?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was born on 28 September 1907.

questions.q: The 1929 Assembly bombing protested the passage of which bills?
options: The Arms and Vernacular Press Bills
The Public Safety and Trade Disputes Bills
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh's Assembly action protested the Public Safety Bill and the Trade Disputes Bill.

questions.q: Which leaflet did Bhagat Singh and Dutt scatter in the Assembly after throwing their bombs?
options: A leaflet declaring 'It takes a loud voice to make the deaf hear'
questions.explanation: They threw leaflets carrying a message that it takes a loud voice to make the deaf hear, explaining their protest.

questions.q: Disguised after the Saunders killing, Bhagat Singh escaped Lahore in 1928 by which means?
options: By train, posing as a gentleman with Durga Bhabhi as his 'wife'
By hiding in a Congress procession
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh escaped Lahore by train disguised as an Englishman's companion, with Durga Bhabhi posing as his wife and a child in arms.

questions.q: Which earlier bombing case in 1929 was Bhagat Singh first convicted of before the Saunders trial concluded?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was first sentenced to transportation for life in the Assembly Bomb Case before being sentenced to death in the Lahore Conspiracy Case.

questions.q: Bhagat Singh briefly worked under a pen name for which Kanpur-based nationalist newspaper?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh wrote for Ganesh Shankar Vidyarthi's newspaper Pratap in Kanpur, often under pseudonyms.

questions.q: Under which pen name did Bhagat Singh write for the newspaper Pratap during his time in Kanpur?
questions.explanation: While working at Ganesh Shankar Vidyarthi's newspaper Pratap in Kanpur, Bhagat Singh wrote under the pen name 'Balwant Singh'.

questions.q: The 1928 reorganization that Bhagat Singh helped shape added which word to the group's earlier name?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh and others added 'Socialist' to rename the Hindustan Republican Association as the HSRA in 1928.

questions.q: Bhagat Singh's father, Kishan Singh, and uncle Ajit Singh were active in which earlier agrarian agitation?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh's family, including his uncle Ajit Singh, were involved in the Pagri Sambhal Jatta peasant movement in Punjab.

questions.q: On what date in 1929 did Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutt throw bombs in the Central Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Assembly bombing took place on 8 April 1929.

questions.q: Bhagat Singh's execution was carried out a day earlier than scheduled. On which date had the hanging originally been set?
questions.explanation: The execution, originally fixed for 24 March 1931, was advanced and carried out on the evening of 23 March 1931.

questions.q: Which collection of Bhagat Singh's writings from prison is a noted source for his political thought?
options: The Story of My Experiments with Truth
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh's 'Jail Notebook' records the books he read and his reflections, illuminating his evolving ideology.
